steps.title: Find the necessity signal in the passage
steps.content: The passage states that managing border environmental matters requires coordination, then explains why: ecosystems extend across the border so actions on one side affect the other, making international cooperation "essential." The blank must mean "necessitates" or "demands" — not merely recommends or describes.

steps.title: Test each word against the necessity signal
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'requires': Means "demands as necessary" — matches the passage's signal that cooperation is essential. ✓
  • Choice B — 'praises': Means to express approval; praising coordination is an endorsement, not a statement of necessity — word meaning does not fit the context. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reports': Means to describe or inform; reporting coordination is neutral and does not convey that it is required — word meaning does not fit the context. ✗
  • Choice D — 'advises': Means to recommend; advising is weaker than requiring and does not match the "essential" signal — word meaning does not fit the context. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
